{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "chrisWalker11", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/51098965?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/chrisWalker11", "id": 51098965, "login": "chrisWalker11", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjUxMDk4OTY1", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/chrisWalker11", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49220", "cvss": {"score": 5.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Impact
A potential XSS attack exists in Jellyfin which can allow a non-privileged user to execute arbitrary Javascript in the context of a logged-in Administrative user, resulting in numerous potential issues. The `Client` header during an `AuthenticateByName` can contain arbitrary HTML and Javascript, which will then be executed by the Administrative user when visiting the `Access` tab of the user in question from within the dashboard.

### Patches
This issue has been fixed in Jellyfin (Web) 10.11.9 via improved sanitization of the Client header.

### Workarounds
N/A

### References

PoC and report provided by Chris Walker (@chrisWalker11).
